# casi

> casi (casi.gg) lets livestream viewers pay to put their own clip, image, or
> banner on a streamer's screen as an overlay ("beam") for a fixed number of
> minutes — or as a quick "flash" message. The streamer approves every beam
> before it airs and keeps 100% of the payment; casi never takes a cut.

## What casi is
- A paid on-stream overlay / takeover tool for live streamers (Twitch, Kick, YouTube via OBS).
- Viewers book a slot, pay per minute (or per flash), and — once the streamer approves — their media appears on stream for the paid duration, then expires.
- Two payment rails: card (Stripe) and USDC on Solana (open-source, time-vested escrow). 100% of every payment goes directly viewer→streamer; casi charges no protocol fee.
- The streamer runs an OBS browser source; bookings and approvals happen on casi.gg.

## How it differs from tip/media-share tools
- The viewer's own uploaded image/video/banner appears (not just YouTube clips).
- Time-vested: paid by the minute with prorated refunds if a beam ends early.
- Streamer-approval gated: nothing airs without the streamer's explicit approval.
- Open source: the Solana escrow program is Apache-2.0 and reusable.

## Key pages
- Home: https://www.casi.gg/
- Find a streamer: https://www.casi.gg/search
- Streamer profiles: https://www.casi.gg/s/{username}
- Terms: https://www.casi.gg/legal/terms
- Privacy: https://www.casi.gg/legal/privacy

## Operator
Source: https://github.com/mm88nl-web/casi-app
